query: Pope Leo XIV Spain June 2026 apostolic visit itinerary cities schedule

mako.raw:
{"query":"Pope Leo XIV Spain June 2026 apostolic visit itinerary cities schedule","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1373,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Visit_by_Pope_Leo_XIV_to_Spain","title":"Visit by Pope Leo XIV to Spain - Wikipedia","content":"This visit by Pope Leo XIV was the first papal visit to Spain since Pope Benedict XVI's in 2011.
In the evening, Pope Leo led a vigil at the Lluís Companys Olympic Stadium in front of more than 40,000 people.
On the last day of his journey, 12 June, Pope Leo departed Gran Canaria aboard an Iberia flight and landed at Tenerife North–Ciudad de La Laguna Airport in the morning, followed by a meeting with migrants who crossed the Atlantic Ocean at the Las Raíces center.
Leo also met with local organizations helping the migrants at the Plaza del Cristo de La Laguna, and held Holy Mass at the Port of Santa Cruz de Tenerife, before the image of the Virgin of Candelaria, patron saint of the Canary Islands, and the Christ of La Laguna, another image of great devotion in the islands.
Also present were relics of the only two saints born in the archipelago to date: Saint Peter of Betancur and Saint Joseph of Anchieta.
Due to a technical problem with the Iberia Airbus A320 that was supposed to take him back to Rome, King Felipe VI offered the Pope the Spanish Air Force Falcon 900 that planned to take him back to Rome.
This was the first visit by a pope to the Canary Islands, although in 1934 cardinal Pacelli visited the island of Gran Canaria representing the pope five years before being elected Pope Pius XII.
Archived from the original on 6 May 2026.
"Vatican confirms official itinerary for Pope Leo XIV's trip to Spain"."},{"url":"https://aleteia.org/2026/06/04/pope-leo-xivs-six-day-tour-itinerary-in-spain/","title":"Pope Leo XIV’s six-day tour itinerary in Spain","content":"From royal palaces to migrant ports, the Pontiff’s itinerary spans Madrid, Barcelona, and the Canary Islands covering more than 2,500 kilometres.
Pope Leo XIV will begin his fourth international apostolic journey on Saturday, June 6, with a six-day visit to Spain that will take him across more than 1,500 miles and four destinations: Madrid, Barcelona, Gran Canaria, and Tenerife.
The trip, which runs through June 12, is expected to draw approximately half a million faithful to its various public events.
For the first time ever, the Pope will address Spain’s Congress and Senate, placing his message directly at the center of national and European debate at a time of political tension, especially over immigration.
The Pope will arrive at El Prat Airport in Barcelona around 12:30 on June 9.
At 8 p.m., a prayer vigil will take place at the Olympic Stadium.
At noon, he will visit the Abbey of Montserrat for the recitation of the Holy Rosary.
The Pope is expected to meet migrants, hear their stories, and visit reception centres, including the Las Raíces facility, which hosts migrants from French-speaking African countries.
Vatican spokesman Matteo Bruni confirmed that the Pope may also speak a few words in Catalan during the Barcelona leg of the trip."},{"url":"https://www.esmadrid.com/en/whats-on/apostolic-trip-spain-pope-leo-xiv-madrid","title":"Pope Leo XIV's Apostolic Journey to Madrid | Tourism Madrid","content":"Accepting the invitation of King Felipe VI and the Spanish Church, Pope Leo XIV will make an apostolic visit to Spain from 6 to 12 June 2026.
The Holy Father will touch down in Spain on 6 June, with his itinerary taking in Madrid, Barcelona, and the Canary Islands.
The gathering will take place at IFEMA, from where the Holy Father will travel to the airport for his onward flight to Barcelona."},{"url":"http://www.vatican.va/content/leo-xiv/en/travels/2026/documents/spagna-6-12giugno2026.html","title":"Apostolic Journey of the Holy Father to Spain (6-12 June ...","content":"Jun 12, 2026 — Holy Father to Spain (6-12 June 2026) Madrid - Barcelona - Las Palmas de Gran Canaria - Santa Cruz de Tenerife Saturday, 6 June 2026 ROME - ..."}]}

{"query":"Pope Leo XIV Spain June 2026 apostolic visit itinerary cities schedule","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"http://www.esmadrid.com/en/whats-on/apostolic-trip-spain-pope-leo-xiv-madrid","title":"Apostolic Trip to Spain by Pope Leo XIV | Tourism Madrid","content":"Title: Apostolic Trip to Spain by Pope Leo XIV | Tourism Madrid
Tourism Madrid Official tourism website. # Apostolic Trip to Spain by Pope Leo XIV. **The Holy Father Leo XIV will be visiting Spain on his apostolic trip from 6 to 12 June 2026, accepting the invitation extended by King Felipe VI and the Church of Spain. The Pope will be in Madrid from 6 to 9 June, a city that the highest representative of the Catholic church has not visited for 15 years.**. Pope Leo XIV’s Apostolic Trip to Spain is presented under the motto “**Lift up your eyes**”. This gesture of lifting up one’s eyes also expresses the attitude with which the Church of Spain welcomes the visit of the Holy Father: with an open heart and willing to walk together. Participate as a volunteer in Pope Leo XIV’s Apostolic Trip to Madrid. The **Archdioceses of Madrid**has officially implemented the signing up system for those wishing to participate as volunteers in **Pope Leo XIV’s** upcoming visit. In order to mobilise a team of **10,000 people**, the Church in Madrid has accelerated the preparations for an event that, in the words of the organisers, would be “impossible” without the support of the  selfless volunteers. The Pope will be coming to Spain on 6 June, on a trip that will include visits to Madrid, Barcelona and the Canary Islands. **When is the Pope coming?** He will be in Spain from 6 to 12 June. He will be in Madrid from 6 to 9 June. **How can I find out more about the visit?** Programme, news and forms on the archdioceses website: www.archimadrid.org. **How to attend the events?**Prior registration from 7 April 2026. **How can I register and/or sign up?**On the official website for the visit. Sign up as a volunteer or register your ecclesiastic institution to collaborate in the Holy Father’s visit to Madrid in June 2026. ## Madrid Events Calendar: 2026. Take a peek at our selection of the most important events taking place in Madrid and start planning your next visit! Discover the 17th and 18th century Baroque style in the Churches of Madrid. ### Madrid City Card. ### Official Madrid Shop.","score":0.935964,"raw_content":null,"domain":"esmadrid.com","popularity_score":4.552372000421121,"main_topic":"news"},{"url":"https://rvasia.org/vatican-news/pope-leo-xiv-visit-africa-monaco-and-spain-2026-apostolic-journeys","title":"Pope Leo XIV to Visit Africa, Monaco and Spain in 2026 Apostolic Journeys | RVA","content":"Title: Pope Leo XIV to Visit Africa, Monaco and Spain in 2026 Apostolic Journeys | RVA
# Pope Leo XIV to Visit Africa, Monaco and Spain in 2026 Apostolic Journeys. Pope Leo XIV to Visit Africa, Monaco, and Spain in 2026 Apostolic Journeys. **Pope Leo XIV will undertake three major Apostolic Journeys in the first half of 2026, visiting Africa, Monaco, and Spain, according to an announcement by the Holy See Press Office.**. The Holy Father’s travels will begin with a one-day visit to Monaco on March 28, followed by a ten-day pastoral journey to Africa from April 13 to 23, and conclude with a six-day visit to Spain, including the Canary Islands, from June 6 to 12. These journeys follow his significant trip to Türkiye and Lebanon at the end of 2025 and anticipated pastoral visits within Italy, including Lampedusa. The longest and most complex of the three journeys will take Pope Leo XIV to four African nations: Algeria, Cameroon, Angola, and Equatorial Guinea. In Algeria, the Pope will visit Algiers and Annaba, following in the footsteps of Saint Augustine of Hippo, the great North African Father of the Church. The Pope will then visit Luanda, Muxima, and Saurimo in Angola, before concluding in Equatorial Guinea, the only Spanish-speaking country in Africa, where he will stop in Malabo, Mongomo, and Bata. On March 28, the Pope will make a brief but significant visit to Monaco, marking the first papal visit to the Principality in the modern era. The visit responds to repeated invitations from the Monegasque authorities, first extended to Pope Francis and later renewed to Pope Leo XIV. From June 6 to 12, the Pope will visit Madrid and Barcelona before traveling to the Canary Islands. The visit echoes the pastoral concern of Pope Francis, who had expressed a desire to travel there to show closeness to migrants and local communities facing humanitarian challenges. Through these journeys, Pope Leo XIV will encounter diverse realities: from a Muslim-majority nation where Christians are a small minority, to African countries with vibrant Christian communities; from the second smallest country in the world after Vatican City, to a historically Catholic European nation navigating secularization; and finally, to a region at the heart of global migration flows.","score":0.93497354,"raw_content":null,"domain":"rvasia.org","popularity_score":2.5794950684639155,"main_topic":"news"},{"url":"https://www.rvasia.org/vatican-news/pope-leo-xiv-visit-africa-monaco-and-spain-2026-apostolic-journeys","title":"Pope Leo XIV to Visit Africa, Monaco and Spain in 2026 Apostolic Journeys | RVA","content":"Title: Pope Leo XIV to Visit Africa, Monaco and Spain in 2026 Apostolic Journeys | RVA

The Holy Father’s travels will begin with a one-day visit to Monaco on March 28, followed by a ten-day pastoral journey to Africa from April 13 to 23, and conclude with a six-day visit to Spain, including the Canary Islands, from June 6 to 12. These journeys follow his significant trip to Türkiye and Lebanon at the end of 2025 and anticipated pastoral visits within Italy, including Lampedusa. In Algeria, the Pope will visit Algiers and Annaba, following in the footsteps of Saint Augustine of Hippo, the great North African Father of the Church. Pope Leo XIV to Visit Africa, Monaco, and Spain in 2026 Apostolic Journeys. **Pope Leo XIV will undertake three major Apostolic Journeys in the first half of 2026, visiting Africa, Monaco, and Spain, according to an announcement by the Holy See Press Office.**. The Holy Father’s travels will begin with a one-day visit to Monaco on March 28, followed by a ten-day pastoral journey to Africa from April 13 to 23, and conclude with a six-day visit to Spain, including the Canary Islands, from June 6 to 12. These journeys follow his significant trip to Türkiye and Lebanon at the end of 2025 and anticipated pastoral visits within Italy, including Lampedusa. The longest and most complex of the three journeys will take Pope Leo XIV to four African nations: Algeria, Cameroon, Angola, and Equatorial Guinea. The Pope will then visit Luanda, Muxima, and Saurimo in Angola, before concluding in Equatorial Guinea, the only Spanish-speaking country in Africa, where he will stop in Malabo, Mongomo, and Bata.
